rationale: Linking verb 'be' in A1 is a structural prerequisite for singular empty subject 'there is'.
Linking verb 'be' in A1 is a structural prerequisite for plural empty subject 'there are'.
Linking verb 'be' is a structural prerequisite for predicative adjectives (be + adjective).
Linking verb 'be' is a structural prerequisite for noun phrase complements.
Singular there is must be mastered before using there is with uncountable nouns in A2.
Singular there is/are supports using quantity expressions like a lot of in A2.
Linking verb be is structural prerequisite for comparative be like clauses.
Present simple copula be is prerequisite for past simple copula use.
Present simple copula be is prerequisite for past simple negative copula use.
Present simple copula be is prerequisite for past simple question copula use.
Linking verb be present in A1 is a structural prerequisite for building future expressions with be going to in A2.
Linking verb be present in A1 is a structural prerequisite for predictions with be going to in A2.
Subject pronouns in statements must be mastered before introducing object pronouns.
Subject pronouns are prerequisite for understanding possessive adjectives.
Possessive adjectives must precede absolute possessive pronoun mine.
Possessive adjectives must precede absolute possessive pronoun yours.
Subject pronouns in statements must be mastered before subject pronouns in questions.
Basic subject pronoun statements are prerequisite for object pronoun 'it'.
Possessive adjectives in A1 support possessive pronoun 'yours' as subject in B1.
Subject pronouns are prerequisite for subject-verb agreement and third-person singular habits statements.
Subject pronouns in statements are prerequisite for present simple negative sentences.
Subject pronouns in statements in A1 are prerequisite for present simple questions in A2.
A1 subject pronouns support A2 reflexive pronouns learning.
Object pronouns in A1 are structural prerequisite for reflexive pronouns in A2.
Determiners with singular nouns are prerequisite for determiners with plural nouns.
Basic singular noun understanding is prerequisite for indefinite/definite article usage.
Articles with nouns are prerequisite for articles with adjectives before nouns.
Article + adjective supports adding adverbs of degree (a very + adjective).
Basic article usage supports prepositional phrases with 'the' + noun.
Using an article vs using no article must be contrasted in A1.
General plural noun determiners in A1 are prerequisite for using 'some' and 'any' pronouns in A2.
Plural determiners in A1 support quantity expressions with 'much/many' in A2.
Plural determiners in A1 support quantity expressions with 'a lot of' in A2.
Plural determiners in A1 support quantity expressions with 'many' in A2.
Indefinite article in A1 is spiraled to definite vs indefinite article contrast in A2.
Singular noun phrases are structural prerequisite for plural noun phrases.
Mastery of main verbs in base form is prerequisite for present simple affirmative.
Present simple affirmative is prerequisite for negative structure using don't/doesn't.
Present simple affirmative in A1 is prerequisite for interrogative structure using do/does in A2.
Present simple form unlocks describing habits and general facts.
Present simple affirmative supports adding adverbs of frequency.
A1 present simple structure supports modifying with 'really' in A2.
A1 habits and routines are spiraled to A2 present simple habits and routines.
Present simple structure in A1 supports learning mental process stative verbs in B1.
Present simple structure in A1 supports reporting verbs in B1.
Present simple structure in A1 supports making suggestions in B1.
Present simple structure in A1 supports giving instructions/directions in B1.
Present simple structure in A1 supports real/imagined situations after 'if' in B1.
Auxiliary verb 'be' is a structural prerequisite for present continuous affirmative.
Present continuous affirmative is prerequisite for its negative form.
Present continuous affirmative is prerequisite for its interrogative form.
Present continuous form unlocks describing active events in progress.
Basic present continuous in A1 supports temporary situations use case in A2.
Present continuous in A1 supports present continuous for future arrangements in A2.
Present continuous in A1 supports future arrangements questions in A2.
Present continuous in A1 supports adding adverbs of indefinite frequency in B1.
Present continuous in A1 supports temporary repeated actions in B1.
Present continuous in A1 supports polite usage in B1.
Present continuous in A1 supports rhetorical questions in B1.
Present simple (habitual) contrasts with present continuous (temporary) in A2.
Main verbs in base form are prerequisite for past simple affirmative.
Past simple affirmative is a prerequisite for negative past structures with didn't.
Past simple affirmative is a prerequisite for questions with did.
Past simple form unlocks describing everyday past events and states.
Past simple affirmative supports complex past clauses with 'when'.
Past simple form supports representing habitual states and actions.
A2 past simple supports ordering of past events in B1.
A2 past simple supports imaginary situations after 'if' in B1.
A2 past simple supports expressing regret in B1.
Past simple in A2 is prerequisite for past continuous in B1.

Past simple in A2 is prerequisite for past perfect simple in B1.
Present continuous in A1 is prerequisite for be going to future form in A2.
Going to future affirmative in A2 is prerequisite for negative form in B1.
Going to future affirmative is prerequisite for questions in A2.
Going to future affirmative form unlocks predictions in A2.
Going to future affirmative form unlocks stating intentions.
Be going to future plan in A2 is prerequisite for will predictions in A2.
Be going to future negative in B1 is prerequisite for will negative predictions in A2.
Be going to future questions is prerequisite for will questions in A2.
Will future form unlocks making predictions with will.

Will future form unlocks fixed plans with will.
Going to future (intentions) contrasts with will (predictions) in A2.
Can ability affirmative is prerequisite for negative can structure.
Can ability affirmative is prerequisite for can questions structure.
Can ability is semantic foundation that unlocks can requests usage.
Can ability in A1 supports can permission usage in A2.
Can ability unlocks understanding of can as general possibility.

Should advice in A2 supports might possibility in B1.
Must (speaker obligation) contrasts with have to (external obligation) in A2.
Question form in present simple in A2 is a prerequisite for pronoun subject questions in A1.
Affirmative questions with be is prerequisite for subject questions with be.
Basic subject questions support object questions with prepositions.

Yes/no questions in A1 are prerequisite for wh-questions in A2.

A1 yes/no questions support A2 yes/no questions.

A2 wh-questions support auxiliary interrogatives in A2.
Yes/no questions in A2 support tag questions in B1.

A2 comparatives support compound adjectives in B1.
A2 comparatives support compound adjectives in B1.
A2 comparatives support superlative clauses in B1.
Predicative vs attributive comparatives contrast in A2.

Prepositional article phrases support prepositions of place in A2.
Prepositional article phrases support complex prepositions in A2.
